name: F5 BIG-IP iRule — SQL Injection Baseline
objective: Detect and block common SQL injection tokens in query strings and request payloads at the BIG-IP edge before they reach origin applications.
risk_mitigated: SQL injection attempts that can lead to data exposure, authentication bypass, or backend manipulation.
potential_side_effects: Search endpoints and analytics interfaces that intentionally accept SQL-like operators may require URI-based exclusions.
tuning_notes: Start by restricting the iRule to login, search, and form submission paths. Add explicit allow conditions for trusted internal IPs and expected query operators if false positives appear.
deployment_notes: Attach to a staging virtual server first, review triggered requests for at least 72 hours, and only then enable on production listeners with a documented rollback path.
monitoring_notes: Track request blocks by URI, source IP, and pool member. Investigate bursts against login and search routes separately from low-volume admin probes.
